messages.content:
Dianne sent a short email to a close colleague to update them on her current progress on a task they are working on. She ends her e-mail with a ‘TTYL’ followed by her name.

 

c) What is the purpose of the email?
The purpose of the email is to update the close colleague on Dianne's current progress on a task they are working on.

Scenario 2

 

Matt was assigned to type the minutes of the meeting to be sent as an email for all attendees. This is so everyone has a copy of the discussion that happened. He ensures that he spells out all the words properly and that his sentences are complete. 

 

c) What is the purpose of the meeting minutes?

The purpose of the meeting minutes is to provide a written record or summary of the discussion and decisions made during the meeting. It is meant to ensure that all attendees have a copy of the discussion and can refer back to it for clarity or reference in the future.

Scenario 3

 

Jen sent a short thank you email to her colleagues. Jen’s colleagues had congratulated her on her first month at work earlier during their meeting. 

 

c) What is the purpose of the email?
The purpose of the email is to express gratitude and thank Jen's colleagues for congratulating her on her first month at work.

David is writing a report for his supervisor to inform them about the tasks he finished for the day. He ensured that all acronyms he used are properly spelled out to avoid confusion.   

 

c) What is the purpose of the report?

The purpose of the report is to inform David's supervisor about the tasks he finished for the day. It serves as a summary or documentation of the work completed, including any relevant details or updates.

2.1 Create your Presentation
Use the information from the Inventory Management Policies and Procedures below to create your PowerPoint presentation:

 

Delish Relish

Inventory Management Policies and Procedures

 

Policy

The Inventory Management Policy and Procedures will be used to ensure that employees follow the standards for conducting inventory management in all areas of the restaurant.

This policy will apply to all Delish Relish employees, including contractors and casual employees who are required to access or record the Delish Relish inventory.

 

Purpose of the Policy

The purpose of the Inventory Management Policy and Procedures is:

·       To ensure that all staff follow the company procedures in taking inventory.

·       To ensure that all staff manage inventory in accordance with the company’s standard procedures.

·       To ensure that all staff follow the company procedures in organising inventory.

 

Procedures:

Inventory management at Delish Relish includes the following:

 

        I.            Taking Inventory

§  Inventory must be taken daily by assigned staff before the restaurant closes.

§  Assigned staff must access the daily inventory log, which records the following information:

o   Date of inventory

o   Item description

o   Quantity on-hand (must be recorded before the business opens)

o   Remaining quantity (must be recorded at the close of business)

§  Count each item in the inventory and record them in the daily inventory log

§  Submit your list to your supervisor to make a report of the inventory.

      II.            Organising Inventory

§  Clean out stock areas before organising the inventory.

§  Place perishables in cold storage. (Fishes, fresh meats etc.)

§  Check all food items for their expiry dates. Throw out any food items that have expired.

§  Take note of what items will be expiring, and when.

 

§  Ensure that the entrance to the food storage area is properly closed after organising.

 

Your slides should include the following information:

Title Page
Introduce yourself
Purpose of the PowerPoint
What is the Inventory Management Policy
What is the purpose of the Inventory Management Policy
What are the procedures for Taking Inventory
What are the procedures for Organising Inventory
Question and Answer 
Note: Your presentation must include appropriate images and animations to enhance your presentation. Present using conventional grammar, industry jargon, every day and some specific vocabulary and routine pronunciation.
I'm sorry, but I'm unable to create a PowerPoint presentation for you. I can only provide text-based assistance.

Task 1.1 Prepare to Read Complaints Policy
Case Study 1

 

Reading the Complaints Policy

Today is your first week working at Evergrande Flowers. The shop sells cut flowers and ornamental plants. It also provides flower delivery service.

 

On your first week in the shop, your manager asks you to read the shop’s complaints policy and procedure to help you deal with complaints from customers. Your manager provides you with a copy of the text and some questions to help you prepare in reading the document.

 

For this task, you must read the Evergrande Flowers Complaints Policy and Procedure below to answer the questions about the workplace text that follow.

 

Evergrande Flowers Complaints Policy and Procedure

 

Policy Statement

 

Evergrande Flowers created this policy to assist staff in handling confidential complaints and resolving complaints as quickly as possible.

 

The shop intends to display the complaint handling policy in our business so that customers are able to lodge a complaint easily.

 

Definition of a Complaint

 

Complaints are defined as any expression of dissatisfaction or grievance made to staff by a customer or member of the public in relation to the shop’s business.

 

Recording a Complaint

 

All complaints made, verbal or written will be recorded in the shop at the time the complaint is made. The complaint will be recorded by the staff member who took the details.

 

When taking a complaint, staff must record the name and contact details of the customer, as well as complete details of the complaint including the date. Details of communication with the customer and any actions to resolve the complaint will be recorded in the same place.

 

Customers’ personal details or details of their complaint must not be divulged to third parties unless the shop has their written consent.

 

Responding to Complaints

 

All people making a complaint will be treated with courtesy. Where possible, complaints will be resolved at the first point of contact. If appropriate to the complaint, shop assistant, the cashier, and any other frontline staff will resolve a complaint immediately. Complaints will be recorded.

 

If the complaints cannot be resolved immediately, the customer will be given a timeframe, a contact person, and details of the shop’s complaint handling process.

 

Informing Customers of Complaint’s Progress

 

Evergrande Flowers aims to resolve all complaints within ten days. Written complaints will be acknowledged promptly.

 

Customers will be given an estimated timeframe at the time they make their complaint. Customers will be informed of the progress of their complaint regularly, especially if there are any delays or changes to what has been agreed.

 

Customers will also be informed of any changes to our flower products or delivery services as a result of their complaint.

 

Escalation of Complaints

 

If the shop cannot resolve the complaint to the customer’s satisfaction, Evergrande Flowers will inform them about where they can take further action (e.g. Department of Consumer and Business Affairs, Equal Opportunity Commission).

 

Procedure

 

Resolving Complaints Procedure

 

The customer must communicate the complaint directly to the desk staff, verbally or in writing
The shop will give written feedback about the action being taken to resolve the complaint within ten days
If within ten days complaint was not resolved, the shop will provide a written acknowledgment of the complaint and an additional time frame for the action being taken
Where a complaint cannot be resolved after the additional time frame, Evergrande Flowers will advise the customer of their right to refer to the Office of Fair Trading relevant in their state/territory
Question at position 3
3
 
1 point 

 
 
Question at position 3 
Q1c.png

 

c) What is the purpose of the document based on the policy statement.

The purpose of the document, based on the policy statement, is to assist staff in handling confidential complaints and resolve them as quickly as possible at Evergrande Flowers. Additionally, the document intends to have the complaint handling policy displayed in the business to make it easy for customers to lodge their complaints.

Question at position 5 
e) Briefly define ‘complaint’ as indicated in the workplace text.
In the workplace text, a 'complaint' is defined as any expression of dissatisfaction or grievance made to staff by a customer or member of the public in relation to the shop's business.
Describe how the headings are emphasised in the text.

In the text, the headings are typically emphasized through the use of bold formatting. This helps to visually separate and highlight the different sections and topics within the document. By using bold text for the headings, it makes them stand out and allows readers to easily identify and navigate through the different sections of the complaints policy and procedure.

Task 2.1 Interpret Information in the Ways to Increase Income Report
Case Study 1

 

Interpret the Ways to Increase Income Report

You are working as a floral designer in Evergrande Flowers, a shop that sells cut flowers and ornamental plants.

 

The flower shop is preparing for its plans for next year. As part of this, your manager asks all staff to review her report on how to increase the shop’s income. The report will be the basis of the shop for expansion of products and service next year.

 

To complete the following task, you are required to review the Ways to Increase Income Report below and answer the questions that follow.

 

 

Ways to Increase Income

 

January 25, 20XX

 

Prepared by:

Elise Gargett

Manager, Evergrande Flowers

 

 

Section 1: Introduction

 

For more than two years, the main income of Evergrande Flowers are the flower products and delivery services given to our various customers. As customer demand grows, the business needs to find new ways to increase their profit to cover the cost of having a larger shop.

 

Purpose of the Report

 

The author aims to suggest new ways in which the shop can increase its income. It shows the results of a survey conducted by the author on how the shop can make more money.

 

Overview of the Report

 

The results of the survey show that the shop should focus more on specific floral needs of customers, such as weddings, birthdays, anniversaries, and funerals. The shop should also conduct in-home consultations, where flower designers can create custom flower arrangements in the customer’s home.

 

Section 2: Findings

 

In 20XX, Evergrande Flowers had an income of $300,000. The income was from:

Flowers sold in the shop
Ornamental plants
Additional delivery service fees
 

The pie chart below shows the distribution of the shop’s sources of income:

 

Q2a.png

 

Products/Services to Increase Shop’s Income

 

To increase its income, Evergrande Flowers should have more products and services. This will give customers more options to choose from. It would then help the shop reach a wider audience as there are more products and services that cater to specific people.

 

The author conducted a survey on the shop’s 15 staff members to identify what products or services the shop can use to increase its income. All of the shop’s staff members participated in the survey, and the results are shown below.

 

Q2b.png

 

The breakdown is outlined below:

7 staff members say that the shop should focus more on special floral needs such as pomanders, garland, and wreath
4 staff members say that the shop should conduct in-house consultations
3 staff members say that the shop should hold special events
1 staff member say that the shop should sell other products such as gardening equipment and flower vases.
 

Section 3: Recommendation

 

Based on the findings of this report, the following recommendations are made:

Target specific floral needs of customers during peak season. This includes selling more specific flower arrangements such as wreaths during holidays, pomanders for children attending events, and garland for decorating pews and doorways.
Floral Designers should conduct in-home consultations where regular customers can avail their help to arrange the flowers in their garden.
Floral Designers should also conduct special events and design workshops to increase the popularity of the shop. This includes popular themes such as seasonal wreath-making and fresh floral design.
Sell more products other than flowers and ornamental plants. Include fertilisers, gardening equipment, and flower vases in the shop.
Question at position 10
10
 
1 point 

 
 
Question at position 10 
b) What were the 3 main sources of income for the year 20XX?

Based on the report, the three main sources of income for Evergrande Flowers in the year 20XX were:

1. Flowers sold in the shop.
2. Ornamental plants.
3. Additional delivery service fees.
